Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiffs characterize their complaint as "clumsy" and suggest that the gravamen of the complaint is based on "City's inability to deliver on its promised solution for the shortfall in square footage or its decision not to deliver on its promise" (Opposition 3:7-8).The complaint, as currently alleged, does not state any causes of action based on such "promise." Plaintiff shall have 10 days to file an amended complaint as suggested in the opposition (see also Yee Declaration, 7:20-22 and 8:9-14) to state cause(s) of action that are not barred by the statute of limitations, have to been released by the settlement agreement, are not required to be addressed by a writ proceeding, and for which exhaustion of administrative remedies is not necessary. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-09-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN CAREY, ESQ., 415.837.3101, FOR PLAINTIFF WALTER YEE.
JILL CANNON AND VICTORIA CANNON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEYS, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted as to all causes of action. As to the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis, Defendant has met its initial to show that Defendant's actions in the permitting process is protected activity under CCP 425.16(e). As to the second prong, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the merits. January 12, 1998, Plaintiffs signed a release of all claims existing or may exist pertaining to the facts set forth in Yee v. CCSF (case no. 980-740) or related to any transactions, occurrences, acts or omissions set forth or alleged in any of the pleading in the that case. Thus, Plaintiffs have not demonstrated that the first through fourth causes of action in the instant action do not relate to the settlement of claims in the prior action. (See Yee Decl., 12.) For the fifth and sixth causes of action, Plaintiffs' claims are barred by Gov. Code 818.4. As the prevailing party, Defendant is entitled to recover his or her attorney's fees and costs from Plaintiffs. (CCP 425.16(c)(1).) The Court awards $18,408 as reasonable fees/costs. Order signed in open court. jUDGMENT signed in open court. Judge: Paul J. Alvarado, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.
